# F.2 doctor predicate — harness-architecture.md drift + README freshness anchors

For `harness-doctor.sh` (F.1 implements verbatim per §F.0.1 — F.2 ships fragments
only; F.1 is this wave's designated integrator).

## Predicate 1 — generated-doc drift (docs/harness-architecture.md byte-equals a fresh regen)

**Check command:**

```bash
bash "$REPO_ROOT/adapters/claude-code/scripts/gen-architecture-doc.sh" --check
```

**RED condition:** exit non-zero — the committed `docs/harness-architecture.md`
no longer byte-equals a fresh run of `gen-architecture-doc.sh` against the
current `manifest.json` (someone hand-edited the doc, or edited the manifest
without regenerating). WARN (not RED) when neither `node` nor `jq` is
available on the machine (graceful degradation — same posture as
`manifest-check.sh`).

**Fixture:** `gen-architecture-doc.sh --self-test` (5 scenarios, includes an
explicit drift-detection scenario `s3-drift-detected-red`) is the mechanical
fixture suite; a committed-doc-matches-regen fixture is GREEN, a
hand-edited-committed-doc fixture is RED. Re-run: `bash
adapters/claude-code/scripts/gen-architecture-doc.sh --self-test`.

**Live result recorded at F.2 ship-time (2026-07-05):** `--check` GREEN against
this repo's actual `manifest.json` (90 entries, 32 blocking) and the
regenerated `docs/harness-architecture.md` committed in the same commit.

## Predicate 2 — README freshness anchors present and ≤90 days old

**Check command:**

```bash
today_epoch=$(date +%s)
max_age_days=90
fail=0
for f in \
  "$REPO_ROOT/README.md" \
  "$REPO_ROOT/adapters/claude-code/README.md" \
  "$REPO_ROOT/adapters/claude-code/attic/README.md" \
  "$REPO_ROOT/evals/README.md" \
  "$REPO_ROOT/neural-lace/workstreams-ui/README.md"
do
  [[ -f "$f" ]] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
  line=$(grep -m1 -E '<!-- last-verified: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} \(doctor-checked\) -->' "$f")
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && { echo "NO-ANCHOR: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
  date_str=$(echo "$line" | grep -oE '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}')
  anchor_epoch=$(date -d "$date_str" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d" "$date_str" +%s 2>/dev/null)
  [[ -z "$anchor_epoch" ]] && { echo "UNPARSEABLE-DATE: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
  age_days=$(( (today_epoch - anchor_epoch) / 86400 ))
  [[ "$age_days" -gt "$max_age_days" ]] && { echo "STALE ($age_days d): $f"; fail=1; }
done
exit $fail
```

**RED condition:** any of the five README files is missing its
`<!-- last-verified: YYYY-MM-DD (doctor-checked) --> ` anchor line, OR the
anchor date is more than 90 days old.

**Fixture:** a fixture tree with all five anchors fresh (GREEN); a fixture tree
with one anchor 100 days old (RED); a fixture tree with one anchor missing
entirely (RED).

**Live result recorded at F.2 ship-time (2026-07-05):** all five anchors present,
dated 2026-07-05 (0 days old) — GREEN.

## Predicate 2b — doctrine/INDEX.md freshness (generator-verified, not a literal anchor)

`doctrine/INDEX.md` is EXCLUDED from Predicate 2's literal-anchor scan
deliberately: it is fully regenerated by `manifest-check.sh --gen-index`
(deterministic byte-for-byte output, verified by that script's own
`s6-gen-index-golden` self-test scenario), and stamping it with a dated HTML
comment would make the generator non-deterministic (the golden-compare
self-test would fail every day even with zero content drift) for no honesty
gain — regeneration IS the freshness proof for a fully-generated file, the
same reasoning `docs/harness-architecture.md` uses (Predicate 1 above). Its
freshness is instead verified by drift, not by age:

**Check command:**

```bash
bash "$REPO_ROOT/adapters/claude-code/scripts/manifest-check.sh" --gen-index >/dev/null
git -C "$REPO_ROOT" diff --quiet -- adapters/claude-code/doctrine/INDEX.md
```

**RED condition:** regenerating `doctrine/INDEX.md` produces a diff against
the committed version — i.e., the committed file is not what the current
manifest would generate.

**Live result recorded at F.2 ship-time (2026-07-05):** this task's own
`--gen-index` re-run found the previously-committed `doctrine/INDEX.md` HAD
drifted (40 lines changed) and re-synced it in the same commit — a live
demonstration of exactly the drift class this predicate catches.

## Predicate 3 — scripts/README.md not required (documented carve-out)

`§F.2` names "scripts/README if present" — `adapters/claude-code/scripts/`
has no README as of F.2 (67 scripts, each self-documenting via its own header
comment per the repo convention observed in `manifest-check.sh` and
`gen-architecture-doc.sh`). This predicate is a no-op (does not exist =
nothing to check) and is recorded here so a future doctor pass does not
mistake the absence for drift.
